title: get_blocking: scoped-thread pattern is correct and must not be replaced with block_in_place
description: get_blocking uses std::thread::scope to spin a new thread with its own tokio runtime when called from inside an existing runtime. block_in_place panics when called from a current_thread runtime (default in #[tokio::test] and many single-threaded services). The scoped-thread pattern works in every configuration because the spawned thread owns an isolated runtime. The build_client() function in aws-sm and aws-kms uses the same pattern for the same reason. The comments document this correctly. Do not replace with block_in_place or Handle::block_on.
